result.stdout:
Description:
  Run your workflows locally.

Usage:
  Runner.Client [command] [options]

Options:
  --workflow <workflow>                                                    Workflow(s) to run. Use multiple times to execute more workflows parallel
  --server <server>                                                        Runner.Server address, e.g. `http://localhost:5000` or `https://localhost:5001`
  -e, --eventpath, --payload <eventpath>                                   Webhook payload to send to the Runner
  --no-default-payload                                                     Do not provide or merge autogenerated payload content, will pass your unmodified payload to the runner
  --event <event>                                                          Which event to send to a worker, ignored if you use subcommands which overriding the event [default: push]
  --env <env>                                                              Environment variable for your workflow, overrides keys from your env file. E.g. `--env Name` or `--env Name=Value`. You will be asked for a value if you add `--env name`, but no environment variable with name `name` exists
  --env-file <env-file>                                                    Environment variables for your workflow
  --var <var>                                                              Variables for your workflow, varname=valvalue
  --var-file <var-file>                                                    Variables for your workflow, filename.yml
  -s, --secret <secret>                                                    Secret for your workflow, overrides keys from your secrets file. E.g. `-s Name` or `-s Name=Value`. You will be asked for a value if you add `--secret name`, but no environment variable with name `name` exists
  --secret-file <secret-file>                                              Secrets for your workflow
  --environment-secret <environment-secret>                                Environment Secrets with name name for your workflow, name=secretname=secretvalue
  --environment-secret-file <environment-secret-file>                      Environment Secrets with name name for your workflow, name=filename.yml
  --environment-var <environment-var>                                      Environment Variables with name name for your workflow, name=varname=valvalue
  --environment-var-file <environment-var-file>                            Environment Variables with name name for your workflow, name=filename.yml
  -i, --input <input>                                                      Inputs to add to the payload. E.g. `--input name=value`
  --input-file <input-file>                                                Inputs for your Workflow
  -j, --job <job>                                                          Job to run. If multiple jobs have the same name in multiple workflows, all matching jobs will run. Use together with `--workflow <workflow>` to run exact one job
  -m, --matrix <matrix>                                                    Matrix filter e.g. `-m Key:value`, use together with `--job <job>`. Use multiple times to filter more specifically. If you want to force a value to be a string you need to quote it, e.g. `"-m Key:\"1\""` or `"-m Key:""1"""` (requires shell escaping)
  -l, --list                                                               List jobs for the selected event (defaults to push)
  -W, --workflows <workflows>                                              Workflow file or directory which contains workflows, only used if no `--workflow <workflow>` option is set
  -P, --platform <platform>                                                Platform mapping to run the workflow in a docker container (similar behavior as using the container property of a workflow job, the container property of a job will take precedence over your specified docker image) or host. E.g. `-P ubuntu-latest=ubuntu:latest` (Docker Linux Container), `-P ubuntu-latest=-self-hosted` (Local Machine), `-P windows-latest=-self-hosted` (Local Machine), `-P windows-latest=mcr.microsoft.com/windows/servercore:ltsc2022` (Docker Windows container, windows only), `-P macos-latest=-self-hosted` (Local Machine) or with multiple labels `-P self-hosted,testmachine,anotherlabel=-self-hosted` (Local Machine)
  -a, --actor <actor>                                                      The login of the user who initiated the workflow run, ignored if already in your event payload
  -w, --watch                                                              Run automatically on every file change
  --interactive                                                            Run interactively
  --trace                                                                  Client Trace of console log events, to debug missing live logs
  --json                                                                   Experimental json logging
  -q, --quiet                                                              Display no progress in the cli
  --privileged                                                             Run the docker container under privileged mode, only applies to container jobs using this Runner fork
  --userns <userns>                                                        Change the docker container linux user namespace, only applies to container jobs using this Runner fork
  --container-daemon-socket <container-daemon-socket>                      Change the docker container socket location that is mounted, only applies to container jobs using this Runner fork
  --container-architecture, --container-platform <container-architecture>  Change the docker container platform, if docker supports it. Only applies to container jobs using this Runner fork
  --keep-container                                                         Do not clean up docker container after job, this leaks resources
  -C, --directory <directory>                                              Change the directory of your local repository, provided file or directory names are still resolved relative to your current working directory
  -v, --verbose                                                            Print more details like server / runner logs to stdout
  --parallel <parallel>                                                    Run n parallel runners
  --no-copy-git-dir                                                        Avoid copying the .git folder into the runner if it exists
  --keep-runner-directory                                                  Skip deleting temporary runner directories
  --runner-directory <runner-directory>                                    Custom runner directory, can be used to avoid filepath length constraints [default: /tmp/inspectra-help-runner.client-3.14.0.8-67d8d46933ec47dba99b78d75b287057/xdg-data/runner.server/a]
  --no-shared-toolcache                                                    Do not share toolcache between runners, a shared toolcache may cause workflow failures
  --no-reuse                                                               Do not reuse a configured self-hosted runner, creates a new instance after a job completes
  --git-server-url <git-server-url>                                        Url to github or gitea instance [default: https://github.com]
  --git-api-server-url <git-api-server-url>                                Url to github or gitea api. ( e.g https://api.github.com )
  --git-graph-ql-server-url <git-graph-ql-server-url>                      Url to github graphql api. ( e.g https://api.github.com/graphql )
  --git-tarball-url <git-tarball-url>                                      Url to github or gitea tarball api url, defaults to `<git-server-url>/{0}/archive/{1}.tar.gz`. `{0}` is replaced by `<owner>/<repo>`, `{1}` is replaced by branch, tag or sha
  --git-zipball-url <git-zipball-url>                                      Url to github or gitea zipball api url, defaults to `<git-server-url>/{0}/archive/{1}.zip`. `{0}` is replaced by `<owner>/<repo>`, `{1}` is replaced by branch, tag or sha
  --github-connect                                                         Allow all actions from https://github.com with an GHES instance
  --github-connect-token <github-connect-token>                            Provide an optional Personal Access Token for https://github.com
  --runner-path <runner-path>                                              Use this specfic runner instead of spinning up the embedded runner
  --runner-version <runner-version>                                        Use this runner version instead of spinning up the embedded runner
  --remote-checkout                                                        Do not inject localcheckout into your workflows, always use the original actions/checkout
  --artifact-output-dir <artifact-output-dir>                              Output folder for all artifacts produced by this runs
  --log-output-dir <log-output-dir>                                        Output folder for all logs produced by this runs
  --repository <repository>                                                Custom github.repository
  --sha <sha>                                                              Custom github.sha
  --ref <ref>                                                              Custom github.ref
  --local-repository <local-repository>                                    Redirect dependent repositories to the local filesystem. E.g `--local-repository org/name@ref=/path/to/repository`
  --list-options                                                           Print a json structure of compatible options
  --token <token>                                                          custom runner token to use
  --version                                                                Show version information
  -?, -h, --help                                                           Show help and usage information

Commands:
  schedule                     Same as adding `--event schedule` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  workflow_dispatch            Same as adding `--event workflow_dispatch` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  repository_dispatch          Same as adding `--event repository_dispatch` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  check_run                    Same as adding `--event check_run` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  check_suite                  Same as adding `--event check_suite` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  create                       Same as adding `--event create` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  delete                       Same as adding `--event delete` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  deployment                   Same as adding `--event deployment` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  deployment_status            Same as adding `--event deployment_status` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  fork                         Same as adding `--event fork` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  gollum                       Same as adding `--event gollum` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  issue_comment                Same as adding `--event issue_comment` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  issues                       Same as adding `--event issues` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  label                        Same as adding `--event label` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  milestone                    Same as adding `--event milestone` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  page_build                   Same as adding `--event page_build` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  project                      Same as adding `--event project` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  project_card                 Same as adding `--event project_card` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  project_column               Same as adding `--event project_column` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  public                       Same as adding `--event public` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  pull_request                 Same as adding `--event pull_request` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  pull_request_review          Same as adding `--event pull_request_review` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  pull_request_review_comment  Same as adding `--event pull_request_review_comment` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  pull_request_target          Same as adding `--event pull_request_target` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  push                         Same as adding `--event push` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  registry_package             Same as adding `--event registry_package` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  release                      Same as adding `--event release` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  status                       Same as adding `--event status` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  watch                        Same as adding `--event watch` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  workflow_run                 Same as adding `--event workflow_run` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  azexpand                     Same as adding `--event azexpand` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  azpipelines                  Same as adding `--event azpipelines` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.
  startserver                  Starts a server listening on the supplied address or selects a random free http address.
  startrunner                  Configures and runs n runner.

  --var-file <var-file>                                                    Variables for your workflow, filename.yml

  --secret-file <secret-file>                                              Secrets for your workflow

  --environment-var-file <environment-var-file>                            Environment Variables with name name for your workflow, name=filename.yml
  -i, --input <input>                                                      Inputs to add to the payload. E.g. `--input name=value`
  --input-file <input-file>                                                Inputs for your Workflow

  -m, --matrix <matrix>                                                    Matrix filter e.g. `-m Key:value`, use together with `--job <job>`. Use multiple times to filter more specifically.   --interactive                                                            Run interactively

  --json                                                                   Experimental json logging
  -q, --quiet                                                              Display no progress in the cli

  --keep-container                                                         Do not clean up docker container after job, this leaks resources
  -C, --directory <directory>                                              Change the directory of your local repository, provided file or directory names are still resolved relative to your current working directory
  -v, --verbose                                                            Print more details like server / runner logs to stdout
  --parallel <parallel>                                                    Run n parallel runners
  --no-copy-git-dir                                                        Avoid copying the .git folder into the runner if it exists

Usage:
  Runner.Client check_run [options]

Options:
  --workflow <workflow>                                                    Workflow(s) to run. Use multiple times to execute more workflows parallel
  --server <server>                                                        Runner.Server address, e.g. `http://localhost:5000` or `https://localhost:5001`
  -e, --eventpath, --payload <eventpath>                                   Webhook payload to send to the Runner
  --no-default-payload                                                     Do not provide or merge autogenerated payload content, will pass your unmodified payload to the runner
  --env <env>                                                              Environment variable for your workflow, overrides keys from your env file. E.g. `--env Name` or `--env Name=Value`. You will be asked for a value if you add `--env name`, but no environment variable with name `name` exists
  --env-file <env-file>                                                    Environment variables for your workflow
  --var <var>                                                              Variables for your workflow, varname=valvalue
  --var-file <var-file>                                                    Variables for your workflow, filename.yml
  -s, --secret <secret>                                                    Secret for your workflow, overrides keys from your secrets file. E.g. `-s Name` or `-s Name=Value`. You will be asked for a value if you add `--secret name`, but no environment variable with name `name` exists
  --secret-file <secret-file>                                              Secrets for your workflow
  --environment-secret <environment-secret>                                Environment Secrets with name name for your workflow, name=secretname=secretvalue
  --environment-secret-file <environment-secret-file>                      Environment Secrets with name name for your workflow, name=filename.yml
  --environment-var <environment-var>                                      Environment Variables with name name for your workflow, name=varname=valvalue
  --environment-var-file <environment-var-file>                            Environment Variables with name name for your workflow, name=filename.yml
  -i, --input <input>                                                      Inputs to add to the payload. E.g. `--input name=value`
  --input-file <input-file>                                                Inputs for your Workflow
  -j, --job <job>                                                          Job to run. If multiple jobs have the same name in multiple workflows, all matching jobs will run. Use together with `--workflow <workflow>` to run exact one job
  -m, --matrix <matrix>                                                    Matrix filter e.g. `-m Key:value`, use together with `--job <job>`. Use multiple times to filter more specifically. If you want to force a value to be a string you need to quote it, e.g. `"-m Key:\"1\""` or `"-m Key:""1"""` (requires shell escaping)
  -l, --list                                                               List jobs for the selected event (defaults to push)

  -w, --watch                                                              Run automatically on every file change
  --interactive                                                            Run interactively
  --trace                                                                  Client Trace of console log events, to debug missing live logs
  --json                                                                   Experimental json logging

  --parallel <parallel>                                                    Run n parallel runners

  --git-zipball-url <git-zipball-url>                                      Url to github or gitea zipball api url, defaults to `<git-server-url>/{0}/archive/{1}.zip`. `{0}` is replaced by `<owner>/<repo>`, `{1}` is replaced by branch, tag or sha
  --github-connect                                                         Allow all actions from https://github.com with an GHES instance

  --container-architecture, --container-platform <container-architecture>  Change the docker container platform, if docker supports it. Only applies to container jobs using this Runner fork

  -v, --verbose                                                            Print more details like server / runner logs to stdout
  --parallel <parallel>                                                    Run n parallel runners

  --git-api-server-url <git-api-server-url>                                Url to github or gitea api. ( e.g https://api.github.com )
  --git-graph-ql-server-url <git-graph-ql-server-url>                      Url to github graphql api. ( e.g https://api.github.com/graphql )

  --git-zipball-url <git-zipball-url>                                      Url to github or gitea zipball api url, defaults to `<git-server-url>/{0}/archive/{1}.zip`. `{0}` is replaced by `<owner>/<repo>`, `{1}` is replaced by branch, tag or sha

commands.payload:
Description:
  Same as adding `--event schedule` to the cli, overrides any `--event <event>` option.

Usage:
  Runner.Client schedule [options]

Options:
  --workflow <workflow>                                                    Workflow(s) to run. Use multiple times to execute more workflows parallel
  --server <server>                                                        Runner.Server address, e.g. `http://localhost:5000` or `https://localhost:5001`
  -e, --eventpath, --payload <eventpath>                                   Webhook payload to send to the Runner
  --no-default-payload                                                     Do not provide or merge autogenerated payload content, will pass your unmodified payload to the runner
  --env <env>                                                              Environment variable for your workflow, overrides keys from your env file. E.g. `--env Name` or `--env Name=Value`. You will be asked for a value if you add `--env name`, but no environment variable with name `name` exists
  --env-file <env-file>                                                    Environment variables for your workflow
  --var <var>                                                              Variables for your workflow, varname=valvalue
  --var-file <var-file>                                                    Variables for your workflow, filename.yml
  -s, --secret <secret>                                                    Secret for your workflow, overrides keys from your secrets file. E.g. `-s Name` or `-s Name=Value`. You will be asked for a value if you add `--secret name`, but no environment variable with name `name` exists
  --secret-file <secret-file>                                              Secrets for your workflow
  --environment-secret <environment-secret>                                Environment Secrets with name name for your workflow, name=secretname=secretvalue
  --environment-secret-file <environment-secret-file>                      Environment Secrets with name name for your workflow, name=filename.yml
  --environment-var <environment-var>                                      Environment Variables with name name for your workflow, name=varname=valvalue
  --environment-var-file <environment-var-file>                            Environment Variables with name name for your workflow, name=filename.yml
  -i, --input <input>                                                      Inputs to add to the payload. E.g. `--input name=value`
  --input-file <input-file>                                                Inputs for your Workflow
  -j, --job <job>                                                          Job to run. If multiple jobs have the same name in multiple workflows, all matching jobs will run. Use together with `--workflow <workflow>` to run exact one job
  -m, --matrix <matrix>                                                    Matrix filter e.g. `-m Key:value`, use together with `--job <job>`. Use multiple times to filter more specifically. If you want to force a value to be a string you need to quote it, e.g. `"-m Key:\"1\""` or `"-m Key:""1"""` (requires shell escaping)
  -l, --list                                                               List jobs for the selected event (defaults to push)
  -W, --workflows <workflows>                                              Workflow file or directory which contains workflows, only used if no `--workflow <workflow>` option is set
  -P, --platform <platform>                                                Platform mapping to run the workflow in a docker container (similar behavior as using the container property of a workflow job, the container property of a job will take precedence over your specified docker image) or host. E.g. `-P ubuntu-latest=ubuntu:latest` (Docker Linux Container), `-P ubuntu-latest=-self-hosted` (Local Machine), `-P windows-latest=-self-hosted` (Local Machine), `-P windows-latest=mcr.microsoft.com/windows/servercore:ltsc2022` (Docker Windows container, windows only), `-P macos-latest=-self-hosted` (Local Machine) or with multiple labels `-P self-hosted,testmachine,anotherlabel=-self-hosted` (Local Machine)
  -a, --actor <actor>                                                      The login of the user who initiated the workflow run, ignored if already in your event payload
  -w, --watch                                                              Run automatically on every file change
  --interactive                                                            Run interactively
  --trace                                                                  Client Trace of console log events, to debug missing live logs
  --json                                                                   Experimental json logging
  -q, --quiet                                                              Display no progress in the cli
  --privileged                                                             Run the docker container under privileged mode, only applies to container jobs using this Runner fork
  --userns <userns>                                                        Change the docker container linux user namespace, only applies to container jobs using this Runner fork
  --container-daemon-socket <container-daemon-socket>                      Change the docker container socket location that is mounted, only applies to container jobs using this Runner fork
  --container-architecture, --container-platform <container-architecture>  Change the docker container platform, if docker supports it. Only applies to container jobs using this Runner fork
  --keep-container                                                         Do not clean up docker container after job, this leaks resources
  -C, --directory <directory>                                              Change the directory of your local repository, provided file or directory names are still resolved relative to your current working directory
  -v, --verbose                                                            Print more details like server / runner logs to stdout
  --parallel <parallel>                                                    Run n parallel runners
  --no-copy-git-dir                                                        Avoid copying the .git folder into the runner if it exists
  --keep-runner-directory                                                  Skip deleting temporary runner directories
  --runner-directory <runner-directory>                                    Custom runner directory, can be used to avoid filepath length constraints [default: /tmp/inspectra-help-runner.client-3.14.0.8-67d8d46933ec47dba99b78d75b287057/xdg-data/runner.server/a]
  --no-shared-toolcache                                                    Do not share toolcache between runners, a shared toolcache may cause workflow failures
  --no-reuse                                                               Do not reuse a configured self-hosted runner, creates a new instance after a job completes
  --git-server-url <git-server-url>                                        Url to github or gitea instance [default: https://github.com]
  --git-api-server-url <git-api-server-url>                                Url to github or gitea api. ( e.g https://api.github.com )
  --git-graph-ql-server-url <git-graph-ql-server-url>                      Url to github graphql api. ( e.g https://api.github.com/graphql )
  --git-tarball-url <git-tarball-url>                                      Url to github or gitea tarball api url, defaults to `<git-server-url>/{0}/archive/{1}.tar.gz`. `{0}` is replaced by `<owner>/<repo>`, `{1}` is replaced by branch, tag or sha
  --git-zipball-url <git-zipball-url>                                      Url to github or gitea zipball api url, defaults to `<git-server-url>/{0}/archive/{1}.zip`. `{0}` is replaced by `<owner>/<repo>`, `{1}` is replaced by branch, tag or sha
  --github-connect                                                         Allow all actions from https://github.com with an GHES instance

  --runner-version <runner-version>                                        Use this runner version instead of spinning up the embedded runner
  --remote-checkout                                                        Do not inject localcheckout into your workflows, always use the original actions/checkout
  --artifact-output-dir <artifact-output-dir>                              Output folder for all artifacts produced by this runs
  --log-output-dir <log-output-dir>                                        Output folder for all logs produced by this runs
  --repository <repository>                                                Custom github.repository
  --sha <sha>                                                              Custom github.sha
  --ref <ref>                                                              Custom github.ref
  --local-repository <local-repository>                                    Redirect dependent repositories to the local filesystem. E.g `--local-repository org/name@ref=/path/to/repository`
  --list-options                                                           Print a json structure of compatible options
  -?, -h, --help                                                           Show help and usage information

  --env <env>                                                              Environment variable for your workflow, overrides keys from your env file. E.g. `--env Name` or `--env Name=Value`. You will be asked for a value if you add `--env name`, but no environment variable with name `name` exists

  --var <var>                                                              Variables for your workflow, varname=valvalue
  --var-file <var-file>                                                    Variables for your workflow, filename.yml
  -s, --secret <secret>                                                    Secret for your workflow, overrides keys from your secrets file. E.g. `-s Name` or `-s Name=Value`. You will be asked for a value if you add `--secret name`, but no environment variable with name `name` exists
  --secret-file <secret-file>                                              Secrets for your workflow
  --environment-secret <environment-secret>                                Environment Secrets with name name for your workflow, name=secretname=secretvalue
  --environment-secret-file <environment-secret-file>                      Environment Secrets with name name for your workflow, name=filename.yml
  --environment-var <environment-var>                                      Environment Variables with name name for your workflow, name=varname=valvalue
  --environment-var-file <environment-var-file>                            Environment Variables with name name for your workflow, name=filename.yml
  -i, --input <input>                                                      Inputs to add to the payload. E.g. `--input name=value`
  --input-file <input-file>                                                Inputs for your Workflow
  -j, --job <job>                                                          Job to run. If multiple jobs have the same name in multiple workflows, all matching jobs will run. Use together with `--workflow <workflow>` to run exact one job
  -m, --matrix <matrix>                                                    Matrix filter e.g. `-m Key:value`, use together with `--job <job>`. Use multiple times to filter more specifically. If you want to force a value to be a string you need to quote it, e.g. `"-m Key:\"1\""` or `"-m Key:""1"""` (requires shell escaping)
  -l, --list                                                               List jobs for the selected event (defaults to push)
  -W, --workflows <workflows>                                              Workflow file or directory which contains workflows, only used if no `--workflow <workflow>` option is set
  -P, --platform <platform>                                                Platform mapping to run the workflow in a docker container (similar behavior as using the container property of a workflow job, the container property of a job will take precedence over your specified docker image) or host. E.g. `-P ubuntu-latest=ubuntu:latest` (Docker Linux Container), `-P ubuntu-latest=-self-hosted` (Local Machine), `-P windows-latest=-self-hosted` (Local Machine), `-P windows-latest=mcr.microsoft.com/windows/servercore:ltsc2022` (Docker Windows container, windows only), `-P macos-latest=-self-hosted` (Local Machine) or with multiple labels `-P self-hosted,testmachine,anotherlabel=-self-hosted` (Local Machine)
  -a, --actor <actor>                                                      The login of the user who initiated the workflow run, ignored if already in your event payload
  -w, --watch                                                              Run automatically on every file change
  --interactive                                                            Run interactively
  --trace                                                                  Client Trace of console log events, to debug missing live logs
  --json                                                                   Experimental json logging
  -q, --quiet                                                              Display no progress in the cli
  --privileged                                                             Run the docker container under privileged mode, only applies to container jobs using this Runner fork
  --userns <userns>                                                        Change the docker container linux user namespace, only applies to container jobs using this Runner fork
  --container-daemon-socket <container-daemon-socket>                      Change the docker container socket location that is mounted, only applies to container jobs using this Runner fork
  --container-architecture, --container-platform <container-architecture>  Change the docker container platform, if docker supports it. Only applies to container jobs using this Runner fork
  --keep-container                                                         Do not clean up docker container after job, this leaks resources
  -C, --directory <directory>                                              Change the directory of your local repository, provided file or directory names are still resolved relative to your current working directory
  -v, --verbose                                                            Print more details like server / runner logs to stdout
  --parallel <parallel>                                                    Run n parallel runners
  --no-copy-git-dir                                                        Avoid copying the .git folder into the runner if it exists
  --keep-runner-directory                                                  Skip deleting temporary runner directories
  --runner-directory <runner-directory>                                    Custom runner directory, can be used to avoid filepath length constraints [default: /tmp/inspectra-help-runner.client-3.14.0.8-67d8d46933ec47dba99b78d75b287057/xdg-data/runner.server/a]
  --no-shared-toolcache                                                    Do not share toolcache between runners, a shared toolcache may cause workflow failures
  --no-reuse                                                               Do not reuse a configured self-hosted runner, creates a new instance after a job completes
  --git-server-url <git-server-url>                                        Url to github or gitea instance [default: https://github.com]
  --git-api-server-url <git-api-server-url>                                Url to github or gitea api. ( e.g https://api.github.com )
  --git-graph-ql-server-url <git-graph-ql-server-url>                      Url to github graphql api. ( e.g https://api.github.com/graphql )
  --git-tarball-url <git-tarball-url>                                      Url to github or gitea tarball api url, defaults to `<git-server-url>/{0}/archive/{1}.tar.gz`. `{0}` is replaced by `<owner>/<repo>`, `{1}` is replaced by branch, tag or sha
  --git-zipball-url <git-zipball-url>                                      Url to github or gitea zipball api url, defaults to `<git-server-url>/{0}/archive/{1}.zip`. `{0}` is replaced by `<owner>/<repo>`, `{1}` is replaced by branch, tag or sha
  --github-connect                                                         Allow all actions from https://github.com with an GHES instance
  --github-connect-token <github-connect-token>                            Provide an optional Personal Access Token for https://github.com
  --runner-path <runner-path>                                              Use this specfic runner instead of spinning up the embedded runner
  --runner-version <runner-version>                                        Use this runner version instead of spinning up the embedded runner
  --remote-checkout                                                        Do not inject localcheckout into your workflows, always use the original actions/checkout
  --artifact-output-dir <artifact-output-dir>                              Output folder for all artifacts produced by this runs
  --log-output-dir <log-output-dir>                                        Output folder for all logs produced by this runs

  --ref <ref>                                                              Custom github.ref
